confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_5186e253-3839-4c42-976d-96ce5d8d169f read back as promoted

impact: Operator receives an ambiguous done signal while capability is not available through the intended substrate.
Prototype artifacts become stale because the next implementation obligation is not mechanically created.
Reviewers must remember to commission continuation manually, increasing operator burden and context burn.
payload.problem: Narada task completion can leave a capability at facade/prototype stage with no immediate continuation task, causing capability drift and false operator expectation that the capability is usable end-to-end.
proposed_capa: When a task intentionally stops at facade, prototype, spike, or design-only stage, closure must require either an explicit no-implementation-continuation-needed rationale or creation of concrete follow-on implementation tasks.
Evidence inspect/review should warn when task language contains prototype/facade/spike and no continuation task relation exists.
Task close should surface continuation obligations before reporting capability-complete wording.
Review template should distinguish scope-complete from capability-complete.
payload.requested_disposition: Treat as CAPA and implementation proposal for Narada task lifecycle/review ergonomics.
payload.summary: Task 62 closed as a typed MCP facade/prototype, but the useful operator question immediately became whether messages can now be sent via MCP. This exposed a process gap: a task may satisfy a prototype/facade scope while leaving the actual implementation path uncreated unless the operator notices and asks.
payload.title: CAPA: facade-stage tasks must spawn implementation follow-up tasks
